title: Fullback (rugby league)

infobox.value: (or **full-back**) is one of the [positions](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions "Rugby league positions") in a [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") football team. Typically wearing jersey number **1**, the fullback is a member of the team's 'back-line' (No. 1-7).[[1]](#cite_note-1) The position's name comes from their duty of standing the furthest back in defence, behind the forwards (8-13), half backs (6 and 7) and the three-quarter backs (2-5).[[2]](#cite_note-2) Fullbacks are therefore the last line of defence,[[3]](#cite_note-3) having to [tackle](/wiki/Tackle_(football_move) "Tackle (football move)") any opposition players and regather the ball from any kicks that make it through their teammates.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It is for this reason that the fullback is also referred to as the **sweeper** or **custodian**.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Being able to secure high [bomb kicks](/wiki/Bomb_(kick) "Bomb (kick)") is a highly sought quality in fullbacks.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Position in rugby league football

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/62/BillyS.jpg/250px-BillyS.jpg)](/wiki/File:BillyS.jpg)

[Billy Slater](/wiki/Billy_Slater "Billy Slater"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_league_team "Australia national rugby league team"), [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland_rugby_league_team "Queensland rugby league team") and [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") former fullback

**Fullback** (or **full-back**) is one of the [positions](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions "Rugby league positions") in a [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") football team. Typically wearing jersey number **1**, the fullback is a member of the team's 'back-line' (No. 1-7).[[1]](#cite_note-1) The position's name comes from their duty of standing the furthest back in defence, behind the forwards (8-13), half backs (6 and 7) and the three-quarter backs (2-5).[[2]](#cite_note-2) Fullbacks are therefore the last line of defence,[[3]](#cite_note-3) having to [tackle](/wiki/Tackle_(football_move) "Tackle (football move)") any opposition players and regather the ball from any kicks that make it through their teammates.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It is for this reason that the fullback is also referred to as the **sweeper** or **custodian**.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Being able to secure high [bomb kicks](/wiki/Bomb_(kick) "Bomb (kick)") is a highly sought quality in fullbacks.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Fullback is also one of the most important positions in attack,[[7]](#cite_note-7) handling the ball nearly every set of six and often running into open space on the [field](/wiki/Rugby_league_playing_field "Rugby league playing field").[[8]](#cite_note-8) Therefore, together with the two half backs and [hooker](/wiki/Hooker_(rugby_league) "Hooker (rugby league)"), fullback is one of the four key positions that make up what is referred to as a team's 'spine'.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Because the fullback makes the most support runs, players in the fullback role complete more very high-intensity running than any other position.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The [Rugby League International Federation](/wiki/Rugby_League_International_Federation "Rugby League International Federation")'s [Laws of the Game](/wiki/Laws_of_rugby_league "Laws of rugby league") state that the 'fullback' is to be numbered 1.[[11]](#cite_note-11) However, traditionally players' jersey numbers have varied, and in the modern [Super League](/wiki/Super_League "Super League"), each squad's players are assigned individual numbers regardless of position.

## Notable fullbacks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fullback_(rugby_league)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Notable fullbacks")]

See also: [Category:Rugby league fullbacks](/wiki/Category:Rugby_league_fullbacks "Category:Rugby league fullbacks")

Fullbacks who feature in their respective nations' rugby league halls of fame are France's [Puig Aubert](/wiki/Puig_Aubert "Puig Aubert"), Australia's [Clive Churchill](/wiki/Clive_Churchill "Clive Churchill") and [Charles Fraser](/wiki/Charles_Fraser_(rugby_league) "Charles Fraser (rugby league)"), Wales' [Jim Sullivan](/wiki/Jim_Sullivan_(Welsh_rugby_league) "Jim Sullivan (Welsh rugby league)") and New Zealand's [Des White](/wiki/Des_White "Des White"). Churchill's and [Darren Lockyer](/wiki/Darren_Lockyer "Darren Lockyer")'s attacking flair as players are credited with having changed the role of the fullback.[[12]](#cite_note-WIP-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fullback_(rugby_league)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]
